
# Taskmaster Loop Progress
# Started: 2026-05-21T06:11:50.351Z

---

## Iteration 16 - Task 18: Scene transition flow via GameState autoload
- Added scripts/game_state.gd (extends Node) as autoload with `current_score: int`, `set_score(score)`, `get_score()`. Registered in project.godot under `[autoload]` as `GameState="*res://scripts/game_state.gd"`.
- Refactored cross-scene score handoff to use GameState instead of the `GameOver.pending_score` static slot:
  - scripts/gameplay.gd: `_on_snake_died` now calls `GameState.set_score(score)` before starting the death timer; removed the `GameOver.pending_score = score` write from `_on_death_timeout`.
  - scripts/game_over.gd: dropped the `pending_score` static var; `_ready` now reads `GameState.get_score()` and feeds it into `set_score(...)`.
- Updated test/game_over_test.gd to seed/clear GameState in `before_test`/`after_test` instead of `pending_score`; renamed the relevant tests (`test_game_state_score_applied_on_ready`, etc.) and dropped the obsolete `pending_score_consumed_after_ready` test (no longer applicable — autoload retains state across scene changes by design, and gameplay overwrites it on each death).
- Added test/game_state_test.gd (4 tests): default zero, set→get round trip, set overwrites previous, `current_score` field reflects setter. Uses the live autoload (no instantiation) since GameState is a singleton.
- main_scene was already `res://scenes/TitleScreen.tscn` from iter 14; Gameplay state already resets per instantiation (no persistent global state besides the score handoff).
- Ran `godot --headless --path . --import` then full suite: game_state 4/4, game_over 12/12, full suite 102/102 pass.

---

## Iteration 15 - Task 14: Game Over screen scene
- Added scripts/game_over.gd (class_name GameOver extends Control): static `pending_score: int = 0` as the cross-scene handoff slot (since `change_scene_to_file` can't pass args to the next scene's `_ready`), `final_score: int`, `restart_requested` signal, `set_score(score)` updates both `final_score` and (if in tree) `$VBoxContainer/ScoreLabel.text`, `_ready` calls `set_score(pending_score)` then resets pending_score, `_input` emits `restart_requested` on the "restart" action, default handler calls `change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/Gameplay.tscn")`.
- Added scenes/GameOver.tscn: Control root + centered VBoxContainer with GameOverLabel ("GAME OVER", 36pt), ScoreLabel ("Score: 0", 24pt), RestartLabel ("Press R to Restart", 18pt).
- Updated scripts/gameplay.gd: in `_on_death_timeout`, set `GameOver.pending_score = score` before `change_scene_to_file(GAME_OVER_SCENE_PATH)`.
- Added test/game_over_test.gd with 13 tests: root type, label texts, font sizes, pending_score applied on ready, pending_score consumed after ready, set_score updates label, gameplay scene path exists, restart action emits signal, non-restart action does not emit. `before_test` resets `GameOver.pending_score = 0` to isolate the static between tests; helper disconnects the default `_on_restart_requested` handler so input tests don't actually swap scenes.
- Reused the signal-indirection pattern from title_screen (default handler + tests disconnect it) for testability.
- Ran `godot --headless --path . --import` then full suite: game_over 13/13, full suite 99/99.

## Iteration 14 - Task 13: Title screen scene
- Added scripts/title_screen.gd (class_name TitleScreen extends Control): GAMEPLAY_SCENE_PATH constant, `start_game_requested` signal connected in `_ready` to `_on_start_game_requested` (which calls `get_tree().change_scene_to_file`). `_input` emits the signal when "start" action_pressed.
- Added scenes/TitleScreen.tscn: Control root (full-rect anchors) with centered VBoxContainer holding TitleLabel ("SNAKE", font_size 48) and PromptLabel ("Press Enter to Start", font_size 18).
- Updated project.godot: `run/main_scene` from non-existent `res://Main.tscn` to `res://scenes/TitleScreen.tscn`.
- Added test/title_screen_test.gd with 8 tests: root type, label texts, font sizes, gameplay scene path existence, start-action emits signal, non-start action (move_up) does not emit. Helper disconnects the default `_on_start_game_requested` handler before each test to avoid actually changing scenes during input simulation.
- Learning: tried `title._start_game = func() ...` first — GDScript doesn't allow method reassignment on instances (the attribute write silently doesn't override dispatch). Switched to a `signal start_game_requested` + default handler pattern that tests can disconnect, then listen for emission. Use signals (not "monkey-patched" methods) for testable side-effect indirection in GDScript.
- Ran `godot --headless --path . --import` (lesson from iter 12) then test suite. Title tests 8/8 pass, full suite 86/86 pass.

## Iteration 13 - Task 15: Gameplay scene integrating Arena, Snake, Food, HUD
- Added scripts/gameplay.gd (class_name Gameplay extends Node2D): orchestrates game loop via `@onready` refs to `$Arena/$Snake/$Food/$HUD/$TickTimer/$DeathTimer`. _ready wires `snake.food_eaten -> _on_food_eaten` (score++, hud.update_score, food.spawn), `snake.died -> _on_snake_died` (stops tick_timer, starts death_timer), `tick_timer.timeout -> _on_tick` (snake.tick), `death_timer.timeout -> _on_death_timeout` (scene change guarded by ResourceLoader.exists).
- Added scenes/Gameplay.tscn: Node2D root with Arena instance, Snake/Food Node2D children with scripts attached, HUD instance, TickTimer (wait_time=0.15, autostart), DeathTimer (wait_time=0.5, one_shot).
- Input handling in `_input`: move_up/down/left/right action_pressed -> `snake.queue_direction(Vector2i.UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT)`.
- Added test/gameplay_test.gd with 9 tests: initial score/label, food spawns in valid cell, food_eaten increments+respawns, multi-eat accumulation, died stops tick_timer / starts death_timer, root is Node2D.
- Design note: chose Timer node (DeathTimer) over `get_tree().create_timer().timeout` so the death-transition delay is freed with the scene — avoids orphaned SceneTreeTimers in tests. ResourceLoader.exists guard means GameOver scene path is optional until Task 16 lands.
- Ran `godot --headless --path . --import` to register new `class_name Gameplay` (lesson from iteration 12). Full suite 78/78 passes.

## Iteration 12 - Task 12: In-game HUD with score display
- Added scripts/hud.gd (class_name HUD extends CanvasLayer): `score: int`, `update_score(new_score)` writes `"Score: N"` to `$ScoreLabel`, `reset()` calls `update_score(0)`.
- Added scenes/HUD.tscn: CanvasLayer root with Label child `ScoreLabel` (offset 10,10 → 200,40; font_size=24; default text "Score: 0").
- Added test/hud_test.gd with 6 tests: initial score/label, update_score behavior, overwrite, reset, CanvasLayer root.
- Gotcha: headless gdUnit4 run failed initially with "Could not find type 'HUD'" — new class_name not yet in global script class cache. Fixed by running `godot --headless --path . --import` once to refresh the cache. Future agents adding `class_name` should run --import before headless test execution.
- Full suite 69/69 passes.

## Iteration 11 - Task 7: Import audio files
- Copied source/audio/{eat_food,death}.wav to snaketaskmaster/audio/ (new dir). Ran `godot --headless --path . --import` to generate .import files; both pull importer=wav, type=AudioStreamWAV, edit/loop_mode=0 (one-shot), compress/mode=2 (IMA-ADPCM).
- Created test/audio_import_test.gd with 8 tests: each .wav loads as AudioStreamWAV with non-empty data, loop_mode == LOOP_DISABLED, .import params confirm edit/loop_mode=0, and AudioStreamPlayer.play() returns playing == true for each.
- Full suite 63/63 passes.

## Iteration 1 - Task 1: Configure project input map
- Added [input] section to snaketaskmaster/project.godot with 6 actions: move_up/down/left/right (WASD+arrows), start (Enter), restart (R)
- Created test/input_map_test.gd with 7 tests verifying action presence and key bindings
- All tests pass (headless gdUnit4 run)

### Learnings
- Godot project must be imported (`godot --headless --path . --import`) before running gdUnit4 headless, otherwise GdUnitTestCIRunner global class is not registered and the cmd tool fails to parse.

## Iteration 2 - Task 2: Import and configure floor tile sprite
- Copied source/sprites/floor_tile.png to snaketaskmaster/sprites/floor_tile.png
- Ran `godot --headless --import` to generate floor_tile.png.import (defaults already: compress/mode=0 Lossless, mipmaps/generate=false)
- Added project setting `rendering/textures/canvas_textures/default_texture_filter=0` (Nearest) for pixel-art rendering
- Created test/floor_tile_import_test.gd with 3 tests: texture loads, .import params correct, project filter is Nearest
- All tests pass

### Learnings
- In Godot 4, the per-texture "Filter Off (Nearest)" pixel-art setting is NOT in the .import file — it's the project-wide `rendering/textures/canvas_textures/default_texture_filter` setting (0=Nearest, 1=Linear default). Per-sprite override is via CanvasItem.texture_filter.

- Created test/wall_tile_import_test.gd with 3 tests: texture loads, .import params correct, dimensions match floor_tile
- All tests pass (3/3)

## Iteration 7 - Task 8: Implement Arena scene with grid-based tilemap
- Created scripts/arena.gd: class Arena extends Node2D with exports grid_width=20, grid_height=15, cell_size=32. Builds TileSet programmatically with floor (source 0) + wall (source 1) atlas sources, populates TileMapLayer with wall perimeter + floor interior in _ready().
- Public API: is_wall(Vector2i) treats perimeter + out-of-bounds as walls; grid_to_world returns cell center; world_to_grid floors world coord into cell index.
- Created scenes/Arena.tscn binding the script to a Node2D root.
- Created test/arena_test.gd with 10 tests covering: default exports, is_wall (corners, edges, interior, out-of-bounds), grid_to_world center placement, world_to_grid floor behavior, round-trip, and tilemap population on _ready (wall vs floor source IDs, tile_size).
- All tests pass (10/10)

## Iteration 10 - Task 11: Implement food spawning logic
- Created scripts/food.gd: class_name Food extends Node2D with const FOOD_TEXTURE preload, position_grid: Vector2i, lazy-created _sprite child, spawn(arena, snake_body) gathers interior cells (1..grid_w-2, 1..grid_h-2) excluding snake_body and uses pick_random(), updates sprite world position via arena.grid_to_world; get_grid_pos() accessor. Empty-valid-cells case returns without mutation.
- Created test/food_test.gd with 6 tests: _ready creates sprite with FOOD_TEXTURE, get_grid_pos returns position_grid, spawn stays in interior bounds (50 iters), never lands on snake body (100 iters), sprite world position matches grid_to_world, deterministic single-cell case (8 of 9 interior cells blocked → always (2,2)). All 6 pass; full suite 55/55.

## Iteration 9 - Task 10: Implement Snake rendering with head and body sprites
- Edited scripts/snake.gd: added HEAD_TEXTURE / BODY_TEXTURE preloads (player_head.png, player_body.png), _sprites: Array[Sprite2D] pool, update_visuals(arena) that grows/shrinks the pool to body.size() and sets each sprite's texture+position+rotation, static _heading_to_angle(h) mapping RIGHT=0, DOWN=PI/2, LEFT=PI, UP=-PI/2. tick() now calls update_visuals(arena) on the non-death path.
- Extended test/snake_test.gd with 7 rendering tests: sprite count matches body, head vs body texture, world positions via arena.grid_to_world, head rotation per cardinal, pool growth/shrink, tick triggers visuals, _heading_to_angle mapping. All 16 tests pass.

## Iteration 8 - Task 9: Implement Snake logic script
- Created scripts/snake.gd: class_name Snake extends Node2D with signals food_eaten + died, exports starting_length=3 and starting_head=(5,7), state body/heading/queued_direction/is_alive.
- reset() rebuilds body horizontally to the left of starting_head (heading right). queue_direction(dir) rejects opposite-heading. tick(arena, food_pos) applies queued_direction, kills on wall or self-collision (per spec), grows on food, pops tail otherwise. Dead snake ignores further ticks.
- Created test/snake_test.gd with 9 tests: starting length, head/heading defaults, queue rejects opposite + accepts perpendicular, tick advances + pops tail, wall collision emits died, self-collision emits died, eating food grows body + emits food_eaten, tick after death is noop.
- All tests pass (9/9)

### Learnings
- After adding a new `class_name X` script that other test files reference, run `godot --headless --path . --import` before the gdUnit4 CLI — otherwise the global class registry isn't refreshed and tests fail to parse with "Cannot infer the type of variable" on any `var x := returns_X()`.

### LLM input
```text
@.taskmaster/progress.txt @CLAUDE.md

Loop iteration 1 of 25

TASK: Implement ONE task/subtask from the Taskmaster backlog.

PROCESS:
1. Run task-master next (or use MCP) to get the next available task/subtask.
2. Read task details with task-master show <id>.
3. Implement following codebase patterns.
4. Write tests alongside implementation.
5. Run type check (e.g., `npm run typecheck`, `tsc --noEmit`).
6. Run tests (e.g., `npm test`, `npm run test`).
7. Mark complete: task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done
8. Commit with message: feat(<scope>): <what was implemented>
9. Append super-concise notes to progress file: task ID, what was done. If there was any mistakes or false assumptions, append them into a learning.

IMPORTANT:
- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.
- If all tasks are done, output <loop-complete>ALL_DONE</loop-complete>.
- If blocked, output <loop-blocked>REASON</loop-blocked>.

```

@.taskmaster/progress.txt @CLAUDE.md

Loop iteration 2 of 25

TASK: Implement ONE task/subtask from the Taskmaster backlog.

PROCESS:
1. Run task-master next (or use MCP) to get the next available task/subtask.
2. Read task details with task-master show <id>.
3. Implement following codebase patterns.
4. Write tests alongside implementation.
5. Run type check (e.g., `npm run typecheck`, `tsc --noEmit`).
6. Run tests (e.g., `npm test`, `npm run test`).
7. Mark complete: task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done
8. Commit with message: feat(<scope>): <what was implemented>
9. Append super-concise notes to progress file: task ID, what was done. If there was any mistakes or false assumptions, append them into a learning.

IMPORTANT:
- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.
- If all tasks are done, output <loop-complete>ALL_DONE</loop-complete>.
- If blocked, output <loop-blocked>REASON</loop-blocked>.

```

- Final result:
```text
Task 2 complete.

**Summary**: Copied `source/sprites/floor_tile.png` to `snaketaskmaster/sprites/`. Godot's default `.import` already produces pixel-art-friendly settings (`compress/mode=0` lossless, `mipmaps/generate=false`). Added `rendering/textures/canvas_textures/default_texture_filter=0` (Nearest) to `project.godot` so pixel art renders crisp at integer scales. Added `test/floor_tile_import_test.gd` (3 tests, all pass headless). Committed as `69f1363`.

Learning logged: in Godot 4 the "Filter Off" pixel-art setting is project-wide (`rendering/textures/canvas_textures/default_texture_filter`), not per-texture in the `.import` file.
```

Loop iteration 4 of 25

TASK: Implement ONE task/subtask from the Taskmaster backlog.

PROCESS:
1. Run task-master next (or use MCP) to get the next available task/subtask.
2. Read task details with task-master show <id>.
3. Implement following codebase patterns.
4. Write tests alongside implementation.
5. Run type check (e.g., `npm run typecheck`, `tsc --noEmit`).
6. Run tests (e.g., `npm test`, `npm run test`).
7. Mark complete: task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done
8. Commit with message: feat(<scope>): <what was implemented>
9. Append super-concise notes to progress file: task ID, what was done. If there was any mistakes or false assumptions, append them into a learning.

IMPORTANT:
- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.
- If all tasks are done, output <loop-complete>ALL_DONE</loop-complete>.
- If blocked, output <loop-blocked>REASON</loop-blocked>.

Builds TileSet programmatically with floor (source 0) + wall (source 1) atlas sources, populates TileMapLayer with wall perimeter + floor interior in _ready().\n- Public API: is_wall(Vector2i) treats perimeter + out-of-bounds as walls; grid_to_world returns cell center; world_to_grid floors world coord into cell index.\n- Created scenes/Arena.tscn binding the script to a Node2D root.\n- Created test/arena_test.gd with 10 tests covering: default exports, is_wall (corners, edges, interior, out-of-bounds), grid_to_world center placement, world_to_grid floor behavior, round-trip, and tilemap population on _ready (wall vs floor source IDs, tile_size).\n- All tests pass (10/10)\n\n## Iteration 8 - Task 9: Implement Snake logic script\n- Created scripts/snake.gd: class_name Snake extends Node2D with signals food_eaten + died, exports starting_length=3 and starting_head=(5,7), state body/heading/queued_direction/is_alive.\n- reset() rebuilds body horizontally to the left of starting_head (heading right). queue_direction(dir) rejects opposite-heading. tick(arena, food_pos) applies queued_direction, kills on wall or self-collision (per spec), grows on food, pops tail otherwise. Dead snake ignores further ticks.\n- Created test/snake_test.gd with 9 tests: starting length, head/heading defaults, queue rejects opposite + accepts perpendicular, tick advances + pops tail, wall collision emits died, self-collision emits died, eating food grows body + emits food_eaten, tick after death is noop.\n- All tests pass (9/9)\n\n### Learnings\n- After adding a new `class_name X` script that other test files reference, run `godot --headless --path . --import` before the gdUnit4 CLI — otherwise the global class registry isn't refreshed and tests fail to parse with \"Cannot infer the type of variable\" on any `var x := returns_X()`.\n\n## Iteration 1"

PROCESS:
1. Run task-master next (or use MCP) to get the next available task/subtask.
2. Read task details with task-master show <id>.
3. Implement following codebase patterns.
4. Write tests alongside implementation.
5. Run type check (e.g., `npm run typecheck`, `tsc --noEmit`).
6. Run tests (e.g., `npm test`, `npm run test`).
7. Mark complete: task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done
8. Commit with message: feat(<scope>): <what was implemented>
9. Append super-concise notes to progress file: task ID, what was done. If there was any mistakes or false assumptions, append them into a learning.

IMPORTANT:
- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.
- If all tasks are done, output <loop-complete>ALL_DONE</loop-complete>.
- If blocked, output <loop-blocked>REASON</loop-blocked>.

2)\n\t\tassert_int(p.y).is_greater_equal(1)\n\t\tassert_int(p.y).is_less_equal(arena.grid_height - 2)\n\t\tassert_bool(arena.is_wall(p)).is_false()\n\n\nfunc test_spawn_never_lands_on_snake_body() -> void:\n\tvar arena := _make_arena(5, 5)\n\tvar f := _make_food()\n\tvar snake_body: Array[Vector2i] = [Vector2i(2, 2), Vector2i(1, 2)]\n\tfor _i in range(100):\n\t\tf.spawn(arena, snake_body)\n\t\tassert_bool(snake_body.has(f.get_grid_pos())).is_false()\n\n\nfunc test_spawn_updates_sprite_world_position() -> void:\n\tvar arena := _make_arena()\n\tvar f := _make_food()\n\tf.spawn(arena, [] as Array[Vector2i])\n\tassert_that(f._sprite.position).is_equal(arena.grid_to_world(f.get_grid_pos()))\n\n\nfunc test_spawn_covers_only_remaining_cell_when_others_blocked() -> void:\n\tvar arena := _make_arena(5, 5)\n\tvar f := _make_food()\n\t# Interior cells in a 5x5 grid: x in [1,3], y in [1,3] -> 9 cells.\n\t# Block 8 of them so only (2, 2) remains.\n\tvar snake_body: Array[Vector2i] = [\n\t\tVector2i(1, 1), Vector2i(2, 1), Vector2i(3, 1),\n\t\tVector2i(1, 2),                 Vector2i(3, 2),\n\t\tVector2i(1, 3), Vector2i(2, 3), Vector2i(3, 3),\n\t]\n\tfor _i in range(20):\n\t\tf.spawn(arena, snake_body)\n\t\tassert_that(f.get_grid_pos()).is_equal(Vector2i(2, 2))\n"

3. Implement following codebase patterns.
4. Write tests alongside implementation.
5. Run type check (e.g., `npm run typecheck`, `tsc --noEmit`).
6. Run tests (e.g., `npm test`, `npm run test`).

8. Commit with message: feat(<scope>): <what was implemented>
9. Append super-concise notes to progress file: task ID, what was done. If there was any mistakes or false assumptions, append them into a learning.

- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.
- If all tasks are done, output <loop-complete>ALL_DONE</loop-complete>.
- If blocked, output <loop-blocked>REASON</loop-blocked>.

- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.
- Keep changes small and focused.
- Do NOT start another task after completing one.

Added `scripts/game_state.gd` as an autoload (`GameState`) with `current_score`/`set_score`/`get_score`, registered it in `project.godot`, and refactored the Gameplay→GameOver score handoff off the prior `GameOver.pending_score` static onto the autoload (`gameplay.gd:_on_snake_died` writes it, `game_over.gd:_ready` reads it). 4 new GameState tests, updated game_over_test seeding, full suite 102/102 pass.

## Iteration 17 - Task 20: Arena unit tests
- snaketaskmaster/test/arena_test.gd already covered most cases (dimensions, perimeter, interior, out-of-bounds, grid_to_world, world_to_grid, round-trip, tilemap population, tileset cell_size).
- Added one missing test from the spec: `test_custom_grid_size_export_vars` — assigns custom `grid_width/height/cell_size` on a fresh Arena and verifies `is_wall` perimeter math and `grid_to_world`/`world_to_grid` honor the new cell_size.
- arena_test 11/11 pass; full suite 103/103 pass.

## Task 19 - Tune gameplay parameters
- Settled defaults: grid 20x15, cell 32, viewport 640x480 (constraint: viewport = grid*cell), tick 0.15s, death timer 0.5s, starting_head (5,7), starting_length 3.
- No code changes - existing values already matched constraints and play well; the missing piece was documentation.
- Added 'Gameplay Tuning (final values)' section to CLAUDE.md with rationale + the viewport=grid*cell constraint future agents must preserve.
- Suite 113/113.

  "new_string": "---\n\n## Gameplay Tuning (final values)\n\nThese values were settled during task 19 (gameplay parameter tuning). Treat them as the project's defaults; if you change one, update this section and check the constraints below.\n\n| Parameter | Value | Where | Rationale |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `grid_width` | 20 | `arena.gd` `@export` (default on `Arena.tscn`) | 18 playable columns after walls — wide enough for sustained horizontal runs, narrow enough that the player must turn often |\n| `grid_height` | 15 | `arena.gd` `@export` | 13 playable rows — keeps the play field landscape (matches 4:3 viewport) |\n| `cell_size` | 32 | `arena.gd` `@export` | Sprite art is authored at 32px; smaller cells look mushy at this viewport |\n| `viewport_width` | 640 | `project.godot` `[display]` | Must equal `grid_width * cell_size` (20 × 32 = 640) — otherwise tiles are clipped or the play area is letterboxed |\n| `viewport_height` | 480 | `project.godot` `[display]` | Must equal `grid_height * cell_size` (15 × 32 = 480) |\n| `TickTimer.wait_time` | 0.15 s | `Gameplay.tscn` | ~6.7 ticks/sec — fast enough to feel brisk, slow enough that a reaction-time turn lands on the next cell rather than overshooting |\n| `DeathTimer.wait_time` | 0.5 s | `Gameplay.tscn` | Long enough to register the death audio + visual before transitioning to game over |\n| `Snake.starting_head` | (5, 7) | `snake.gd` `@export` | Left-of-center horizontally, vertically centered. Snake starts facing right with body at (5,7),(4,7),(3,7), giving ~13 cells of runway before the right wall — enough to read the screen and pick a direction. Tests hardcode this position; bump them together if you move it |\n| `Snake.starting_length` | 3 | `snake.gd` `@export` | Classic Snake starting length; anything longer crowds the small grid |\n\n**Constraint to preserve:** `viewport_width == grid_width * cell_size` and `viewport_height == grid_height * cell_size`. If you tune the grid, also resize the viewport in `project.godot` or you'll get visible clipping/letterboxing.\n\n---\n\n## Workflow Orchestration"

Without templates, the CLI export creates a partial `.tmp` file and exits with code 255.\n- **CLI export** (after templates are installed):\n  ```powershell\n  godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster --export-release \"Web\" \"build/web/index.html\"\n  ```\n- **Local browser test:** HTML5 builds need an HTTP server (file:// won't work due to CORS). [Loop 25] Task 25 done: Added Windows Desktop preset to export_presets.cfg (preset.1, embed_pck=true, x86_64, product_name=Snake, export_path=build/windows/snake.exe). Verified by CLI export - snake.exe (~106MB) produced. Documented in CLAUDE.md. Learnings: (1) Godot CLI export errors 'export path doesn't exist' if the output directory is missing - mkdir first. (2) Post-export editor SIGSEGV is harmless when embed_pck=true on this Godot 4.6.2 build - exe is fully written before the crash; check file size to confirm.

- Complete ONLY ONE task per iteration.

From `snaketaskmaster/build/web/`:\n  ```powershell\n  python -m http.server 8000\n  # then open http://localhost:8000/index.html\n  ```\n\n---\n\n## Windows Desktop Export\n\nThe `Windows Desktop` preset is defined in `snaketaskmaster/export_presets.cfg` and writes to `snaketaskmaster/build/windows/snake.exe`. PCK is embedded (`binary_format/embed_pck=true`), so the export produces a single self-contained `.exe`. `build/` is gitignored and `*.exe` is independently gitignored.\n\n- **Export templates are required** (same caveat as the Web preset). Install via Editor → Manage Export Templates.\n- **The output directory must exist before exporting.** The CLI export errors with \"The given export path doesn't exist\" if `build/windows/` is missing. Create it first:\n  ```powershell\n  New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force snaketaskmaster\\build\\windows\n  godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster --export-release \"Windows Desktop\" \"build/windows/snake.exe\"\n  ```\n- **A post-export editor-process crash (SIGSEGV) is harmless** in this configuration — the `.exe` is fully written before the crash. Verify `snake.exe` exists and is ~100 MB before assuming the export failed. The bash wrapper reports `EXIT: 0` despite the backtrace.\n- **Manual verification:** double-click `snake.exe` and confirm the title screen appears, controls respond, audio plays, and the full gameplay loop runs to game-over. No Godot install is required to run the embedded build.\n\n---"
